answer:
\boxed{B}

Heat engines, like jet engines, operate on thermodynamic cycles where higher temperatures increase the thermal efficiency (as per the Carnot efficiency formula, \( \eta = 1 - \frac{T_{\text{cold}}}{T_{\text{hot}}} \)). Higher temperatures expand the working fluid more forcefully, improving power output and efficiency. While pressure also plays a role, temperature is the primary factor for maximizing efficiency.
